title: Getting Started with NanoQuRe
text: NanoQuRe provides quality control tools Oxford Nanopore sequencing data. Starting sequencing summary file produced ONT basecallers (Guppy Dorado), NanoQuRe generates interactive plots summary tables — can bundle everything self-contained HTML report single function call.

title: Getting Started with NanoQuRe
text: NanoQuRe expects data frame columns produced ONT basecallers. minimum required columns : Loading file straightforward: vignette use example dataset bundled package:
code: seq_summary <- read.csv("sequencing_summary.txt", sep = "\t")  # passes_filtering comes in as a string from some basecallers — coerce if needed seq_summary$passes_filtering <- as.logical(seq_summary$passes_filtering) library(NanoQuRe)  data(sample_data)

title: Getting Started with NanoQuRe
text: main entry point generate_report(). inspects many unique sample_id values data automatically picks right template — single-sample multi-sample — renders self-contained HTML file. rendered file open browser automatically contains plots summary tables covering read quality, yield, throughput, pore activity.

title: Getting Started with NanoQuRe
text: multiple samples, bind data frames together calling generate_report(). function detects one unique sample_id switches multi-sample template automatically:

title: Getting Started with NanoQuRe
text: report bundles output individual NanoQuRe functions. want explore customise single plot, Using Individual Functions vignette covers one detail.

title: Using Individual Functions
text: generate_report() bundles everything one HTML file, every plot table NanoQuRe also available standalone function. useful want embed specific plot R Markdown report, tweak parameters, inspect single metric interactively.

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Returns one-row summary run: duration, total bases sequenced, pass rate, average translocation speed.
code: sequencing_stats(sample_data) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   `sample id`     `duration [h]` `number of reads` `total bases sequenced [Gb]` #>   <chr>                    <dbl>             <int>                        <dbl> #> 1 experiment_0001           88.6              1000                        0.001 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: `passed reads [%]` <dbl>, `average speed [bp/s]` <dbl>

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Returns key quality metrics: total passed read counts, N50, mean Q score, longest read, mean length passed reads.
code: quality_stats(sample_data) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   `sample id`     `all reads` `passed reads` `n50 value` `mean qscore` #>   <chr>                 <int>          <int>       <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1 experiment_0001        1000            885        1201          10.9 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: `longest read` <dbl>, `passed mean length` <dbl>

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Shows total sequenced bases accumulate run, split pass/fail status. flattening curve indicates run winding .

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Shows yield per hour rather cumulative yield — useful spotting periods low pore activity blockages run.

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Shows average translocation speed DNA pore bases per second, split pass/fail. drop speed can indicate pore aging buffer issues.

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Plots read length distribution vertical lines mean read length N50 value. upper_limit parameter controls x axis maximum — useful small number long reads compress rest distribution.
code: # default upper limit is 4000 bp plot_read_lengths(sample_data) # zoom out to see the full long-read tail plot_read_lengths(sample_data, upper_limit = 20000)

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Plots Q score distribution split pass/fail. Two vertical lines shown: basecaller’s pass/fail threshold (derived data) user-defined cutoff downstream analysis.
code: # default user-defined cutoff is Q7 plot_quality_distribution(sample_data, qscore_cutoff = 7)

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Shows many pore channels remain active course run. steep early drop suggests many pores became blocked inactive quickly.

title: Using Individual Functions
text: Shows two complementary views pore activity: time × channel heatmap spatial heatmap overlaid flowcell layout. Darker colour means bases sequenced channel.
